instruction:
The x-coordinate of a particle moving on x-axis is given by $x = 3 sin 100t + 8 cos^2 50t$, where x is in cm and t is time in seconds. Which of the following is/are correct about this motion?

Options:
A. the amplitude of the resultant S.H.M. is $\sqrt{65}$ cm
B. the maximum displacement of the particle from the origin is $12$ cm.
C. the amplitude of the S.H.M. of the particle is $4$ cm
D. the amplitude of the resultant S.H.M. is  $\sqrt{73}$ cm
E. the maximum displacement of the particle from the origin is $9$ cm.
F. the maximum displacement of the particle from the origin is $7$ cm.
G. the maximum displacement of the particle from the origin is $10$ cm.
